narrative:
Pinebrook Advisory Group is a five-person management consultancy serving
mid-market operating companies. The firm takes on a handful of engagements
a year: operational reviews, pricing studies, and post-acquisition
integration support. Its documents are lean and practical: engagement
letters on firm letterhead, kickoff memos, meeting minutes that name every
attendee, and status reports written for a client executive who skims.
The Managing Partner signs everything that leaves the building. Tone is
plain American business English, first names inside the firm, full names
and titles when a client is on the page.
instructions:
Author every document briefed in `docs`, as JSON matching the deliverable
schema:

  {"schema_id": "orgsmith/authoring-deliverable@1",
   "work_order_id": "wo:author:0001",
   "docs": [{"schema_id": "orgsmith/docir@1", "doc_id": "d:....",
             "blocks": [...]}, ...]}

Block kinds: heading (text, level), paragraph (text), list (items),
table (header, rows), sigblock (signers = person ids from the brief).

Hard rules:
- One DocIR per brief; doc_ids must match the work order exactly.
- Every fact id briefed for a doc MUST appear as a literal placeholder
  {{fact:<id>}} at least once in that doc's text. You do not know fact
  values; never write a number, date, or name in place of a placeholder.
- Use only placeholders for fact ids briefed on that document.
- Do not invent people, organizations, addresses, amounts, or dates.
  People and their titles come from the briefs; the org narrative sets
  tone only.
- Write plain, era-appropriate business prose in the org's voice.

docs.title: Engagement Letter: Operational Review for Weaver-Reeves

authors.name: Chad Miller
authors.title: Managing Partner
authors.persona: Chad Miller built Pinebrook after a long operating career and it shows: he writes the way he ran plants, short declarative sentences, numbers up front, one ask per letter. He signs everything that leaves the firm and edits juniors' drafts by crossing out adjectives. Clients get his cell number and he answers it.

participants.name: Jenna Wiley
participants.title: Principal Consultant
participants.persona: Jenna Wiley runs delivery on most engagements and writes the firm's kickoff memos and status reports. Structured to a fault: every document she touches gets a numbered workstream list and an owner beside every action. Warm on the phone, terse on the page. Allergic to consultant jargon; says 'what changed and what it costs' at least once a week.

participants.name: Lisa Hernandez
participants.title: Analyst
participants.persona: Lisa Hernandez is the analyst on the team and the firm's default minute-taker. She captures meetings almost verbatim, names every attendee, and keeps a running action list that she chases without being asked. Her writing is neutral and precise; when she is unsure she writes 'to confirm' rather than guessing.

docs.engagement_summary: Operational Review for Weaver-Reeves, running about 3 months. Commercial terms and dates are briefed as fact placeholders only.

facts.hint: amount of money; place where the figure belongs
calendar date; place where the date belongs
proper name; place where the name belongs

docs.guidance: Formal letter on firm letterhead confirming the engagement: scope, approach, team, fee and start date, closing. End with a sigblock block signed by the letter's author and the client contact. 3-6 paragraphs.
